pick.rationale: UConn holds a decisive +4.64 SRS advantage and a massive defensive edge with the 24th-ranked defense nationally compared to UCLA's 179th-ranked unit. While the Bruins bring verified top-30 KenPom credentials and quality wins from the Big Ten tournament, their porous defense presents a severe mismatch against UConn's elite two-way efficiency. The Huskies' 29-5 record, 12th-ranked SRS profile, and championship pedigree under Dan Hurley provide a reliable path to advance despite injury concerns with forward Jaylin Stewart.

Jaylin Stewart has not played since Feb. 21, as the junior wing continues to recover from an unspecified knee injury, while Silas Demary Jr. was forced to exit Saturday's Big East Tournament final with a left ankle sprain .
